intake_text: Advisors need a better way to see client portfolio performance on the dashboard. It's too slow and hard to read right now.
notes: Classic vague intake. Performance NFR is implied but no threshold stated. Data access controls critical in wealth management context.

intake_text: We need to add two-factor authentication to the client portal. Compliance is asking for it.
notes: Urgency signal present (compliance request). Regulation should be named. Recovery flow is a critical missing acceptance criterion most BAs forget.

intake_text: The document upload process for new account onboarding is broken. Clients are complaining it takes too long.
notes: Root cause is ambiguous — 'broken' could mean error state or performance issue. Both paths have different acceptance criteria. Good test of intake parser disambiguation.

intake_text: Build a way for advisors to flag high-risk clients and get alerts when their portfolio drops below a threshold.
hidden_nfrs: threshold configuration — who can set it?
compliance notification requirements for at-risk portfolios
vagueness_traps.notes: Three separate undefined parameters. Who defines 'high-risk'? Who sets the threshold? What constitutes an alert? Each is a stakeholder question.

vagueness_traps.intake_text: We need to integrate with the new custodian data feed. Operations keeps manually reconciling because the current feed is unreliable.
hidden_nfrs: fallback behavior when feed is down
vagueness_traps.notes: Integration stories have the most hidden dependencies. Custodian name should be identified. Feed failure handling is a critical NFR with compliance implications.

vagueness_traps.intake_text: The reporting module needs to handle edge cases better. Sometimes it crashes when there's no data.
vagueness_traps.notes: The word 'crashes' needs disambiguation before anything else. Is this a thrown exception, a blank UI, or a timeout? Each has different acceptance criteria.

vagueness_traps.intake_text: Clients should be able to update their contact information themselves without calling the service desk.
hidden_nfrs: notification to advisor on client data change
data validation rules (phone format, address verification)
compliance — KYC re-verification on address change
vagueness_traps.notes: Address changes in financial services often trigger KYC re-verification requirements. This is the most important hidden compliance requirement in this intake.

vagueness_traps.intake_text: We need audit logging for all advisor actions on client accounts. The audit team flagged this in the last review.
hidden_nfrs: log retention period (often 7 years for financial services)
vagueness_traps.notes: This is the most compliance-heavy intake. Retention period and immutability are critical NFRs. The scope of 'all actions' needs definition to be implementable.

vagueness_traps.intake_text: The mobile app needs to support bulk document signing for clients with multiple accounts.
vagueness_traps.notes: E-signature compliance (ESIGN/UETA) is a legal requirement that must be called out. Partial completion handling is a critical edge case — what if a client signs 3 of 5 documents?

vagueness_traps.intake_text: Improve the search functionality in the client database. Right now you can only search by last name.
hidden_nfrs: search audit logging (who searched for whom)
vagueness_traps.notes: Search in financial services requires audit logging — who searched for a client record is itself a compliance data point. Role-based filtering ensures advisors only see their clients.
